description: Wave 13 - 'Make Solo Play Real: mobile-first quickplay vs AI' (DRAFT, not run). Finalized after an adversarial review (frontend+backend claims verified against code). TODAY the friends-group lobby loop works end-to-end (create lobby -> invite link -> guests join -> ready -> launch -> play -> spot-the-AI reveal), the real-time SSE/spectator stack is excellent, and the scientific firewall is ironclad (human games write ZERO canonical rating rows; composition is counts-only/anonymous-by-default). GAPS this wave closes: (1) a lone visitor cannot start a game (no solo 'find a match vs AI' entry; home has no Play CTA; the curated matchmaker + core/lobby/autofill.py are unwired to a human-facing solo route); (2) the signed-in /profile is BROKEN - web client calls GET /human/stats (client.ts:339) but api/routes/human.py serves no such route, even though data is materialized in human_player_stats (analytics/human_stats.py, migration 0034); (3) mobile is a LAUNCH REQUIREMENT but the play surface is a fixed desktop 3-column grid (md:grid-cols-[220px_1fr_300px]); (4) core game-feel is thin (no live vote-tally though the play-state reducer tracks VoteSubmitted, no eliminated/phase feedback, no connection indicator, no onboarding). PRODUCT north star: consumer game AND research instrument unified by the imitation game - every human-vs-AI session is both a play session and a human-baseline Turing datapoint. The wave makes a lone visitor: land -> learn in one screen -> 'Play vs AI' -> matched + AI-autofilled in seconds (mobile-playable) -> finish with real game-feel -> spot-the-AI reveal -> see real /profile stats. KEY ENGINEERING FACTS from the review: (a) a human-vs-AI game is a HUMAN-LANE game (has a HUMAN seat) advanced ONLY by the SEPARATE run_human_lane worker (`padrino human-lane`), NOT run_scheduler; the e2e smoke harness today spawns only serve+scheduler, so US-288 must extend it to also run the human-lane worker AND give it a deterministic mock-AI seam before US-287's funnel test can pass. (b) There is NO game-level launch primitive: launch_lobby (api/lobby_launch.py) is lobby-keyed (requires a LOCKED lobby with exactly ruleset.PLAYER_COUNT seats), so POST /human/match must compose a single-human lobby (create_lobby -> HUMAN seat 0 -> AI seats 1..N -> lock -> launch_lobby), reusing autofill_empty_seats (NOT matchmaker.next_match, which seats EVERY seat incl the human). (c) The dashboard has NO Svelte component-test harness (vitest 'include' is TS/JS only; no @testing-library/svelte): reducer/derivation logic is tested via vitest over synthetic frames (extend playState.test.ts / liveClient.test.ts), and render / narrow-viewport / live-SSE behavior is tested via Playwright e2e with page.setViewportSize + route-mocked observation/live SSE fixtures (the web/dashboard/tests/e2e/play.spec.ts pattern). (d) The profile page gates stats on me.kind==='account'; since the solo funnel mints a GUEST, US-286 must un-gate guest stats (backend already serves by principal_id). SCOPE GUARDS: solo matches are CASUAL human-vs-AI (no stranger-to-stranger chat -> no new moderation scope); write ZERO canonical rating rows; ranked human ELO stays DORMANT (Wave 14 hardens anti-cheat + consent before ranked switch-on). Hard rules: pure core (SeededRng, injected clocks; deterministic autofill/role-assignment replay bit-for-bit), identity-blind anonymous-by-default (AGENTS.md rule 7), human/scientific segregation, NEVER edit shipped migrations. Frontend-heavy (SvelteKit) - dashboard stories MUST pass the pnpm gates (pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check, and e2e where the AC names it). OUT OF SCOPE: public stranger matchmaking; stranger chat/moderation; ranked ELO switch-on + anti-smurf/decay (Wave 14); friends graph / rematch / host-control UI (Wave 15); cosmetics/monetization; native mobile apps (responsive web only). DRAFT: 13 stories US-276..288.

userStories.title: Implement GET /human/stats (project materialized counts into the client HumanPlayerStats shape)
userStories.description: BLOCKER (backend). The web client calls GET /human/stats (web/dashboard/src/lib/api/client.ts:338-340 getHumanStats -> request('/human/stats',{ruleset_id})) but api/routes/human.py serves NO such route, so the call 404s. The data is materialized: human_player_stats (db model + Alembic 0034) populated by analytics/human_stats.py. CRUCIAL: human_player_stats stores raw COUNTS + JSON (games, wins, survived_games, voting_total_votes/voting_accurate_votes, detection_total/detection_accurate, role_win_rates_json, faction_win_rates_json), but the client HumanPlayerStats type (web types.ts ~484) expects DERIVED fields with different names/shapes (survival_rate: number; voting_accuracy: {total_votes, accurate_votes, rate}; detection_accuracy: string; role_win_rates: items {role, wins, games, rate} - note the stored JSON uses key 'name' where the client expects 'role'). So the route must PROJECT the stored counts into the exact client shape. Add an authenticated GET /human/stats route (use require_human / the same auth the other /human routes use, which admits guests) returning the caller's casual stats projected from human_player_stats, framed CASUAL (no rating/standings). NOTE: this is the SERVER route only - it does NOT by itself make the funnel work, because the profile page also gates rendering on account-kind (un-gated in US-286).
acceptanceCriteria: GET /human/stats?ruleset_id=... returns 200 with the caller's casual stats projected into the EXACT client HumanPlayerStats shape (types.ts): survival_rate = survived_games/games (0.0 when games==0); voting_accuracy = {total_votes, accurate_votes, rate}; detection_accuracy as the string the client's fraction-parser expects; role_win_rates parsed from role_win_rates_json remapping stored 'name' -> client 'role' as {role, wins, games, rate}; a test asserts the projected shape + values for a seeded human (and the name->role remap).

Auth uses require_human (admits both guest and account principals); an unauthenticated request is rejected (401); a human with no games returns a well-formed zero/empty payload (not 404/500); a test asserts both.

Stats derive from the materialized human_player_stats projection (analytics/human_stats.py), not a hot-path recompute beyond the existing refresh contract; a test asserts projected values match analytics/human_stats output.
Firewall: only casual human stats are exposed, never any canonical scientific rating; a test asserts no scientific rating fields are returned.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: src/padrino/api/routes/human.py (ADD route), web/dashboard/src/lib/api/client.ts:338-340 + web types.ts (~484 HumanPlayerStats type - the projection target), src/padrino/analytics/human_stats.py (refresh/projection), human_player_stats model + Alembic 0034. require_human admits guests (needed - the solo funnel is guest-based). Backend-only story (no UI change here - the profile guest-gate is US-286). Watch the 'name'->'role' key remap in role_win_rates_json.

userStories.title: Solo instant-match endpoint POST /human/match (compose single-human lobby -> autofill AI -> launch -> game_id)
userStories.description: BLOCKER (backend). No solo 'find a match vs AI' path exists. There is NO game-level launch primitive: launch_lobby (api/lobby_launch.py:~90) is LOBBY-keyed - it requires an existing Lobby with status LOCKED and exactly ruleset.PLAYER_COUNT seats, then materializes the Game with deterministic AI fill (autofill_empty_seats), assign_roles, and identity-blind composition. So add an authenticated POST /human/match that COMPOSES a single-human CASUAL lobby and reuses the lobby launch path: enforce_consent (TOS+Privacy+16+) -> create_lobby (host = current human principal; mint/reuse a guest session like other /human routes) -> add HUMAN seat at index 0 + AI seats 1..N-1 -> lock (OPEN->LOCKED) -> launch_lobby(session, lobby_id=...) -> return {game_id} for the client to route to /play/{gameId}. Reuse autofill_empty_seats + the curated human_eligible_pool (human_cost_governance.human_eligible_pool / lobby_launch._curated_roster). Do NOT call matchmaking/matchmaker.next_match (it seats EVERY seat incl the human and has no HUMAN-seat concept). Honor admission/cost governance: ACTION_CREATE and ACTION_LAUNCH each consume the same per-user/day game slot, so mirror the lobbies launch route (release_admission_for_lobby between create and re-admit at launch) to avoid double-counting. Produce an ANONYMOUS identity-blind game (counts-only, AGENTS.md rule 7) that writes ZERO canonical rating rows.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /human/match (authenticated; mints/reuses a guest session) enforces consent (TOS+Privacy+16+), composes a single-human CASUAL lobby (HUMAN seat 0, AI seats 1..N-1), locks it, and reuses launch_lobby to materialize+launch the game, returning {game_id}; a test asserts the created game has exactly ONE HUMAN seat and the rest AI (via autofill_empty_seats, deterministic/replayable for the same seed) and is launched.
Consent is enforced before any game is materialized: a caller without valid consent is refused (not 500); a test asserts the consent gate.

Admission + cost governance: a caller over the per-user/day game cap or when the global cost breaker is open is refused with a clear status; the ACTION_CREATE/ACTION_LAUNCH double-count is avoided (release-between as the lobbies launch route does); a test asserts the cap/breaker refusal AND that a single match consumes exactly one game slot (not two).

The created game is ANONYMOUS identity-blind (counts-only; no per-seat human/AI map pre-reveal) and CASUAL (zero canonical scientific rating rows); a test asserts the segregation (zero scientific rating rows) and identity-blind composition.
All backend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration').
userStories.notes: Sites: api/routes/human.py (ADD POST /human/match), api/lobby_launch.py (launch_lobby is lobby-keyed; reuse - do NOT hand-roll Game/GameSeat materialization), api/routes/lobbies.py (mirror its launch route: enforce_consent + release_admission_for_lobby + re-admit ACTION_LAUNCH), core/lobby/autofill.py (autofill_empty_seats - the CORRECT fill primitive, preserves the HUMAN seat), economics/human_cost_governance.py (admission caps/breaker + human_eligible_pool curated pool). NAME 'match' (the existing 'guest quickplay'/POST /human/guest mints a SESSION, not a game). Do NOT use matchmaker.next_match (seats all seats) and do NOT route through scientific continuous_matchmaking. Casual + anonymous + firewall-safe.

userStories.title: Home 'Play vs AI' CTA + one-tap entry (guest mint + consent inline -> match -> /play)
userStories.description: The home page (web/dashboard/src/routes/+page.svelte) and nav (+layout.svelte) offer NO way for a lone visitor to start a game (only leaderboard/ladder/watch links; nav 'Play' opens the friends lobby). Add a prominent 'Play vs AI' CTA on the home page (and a distinct nav entry) that takes a first-timer into a solo game in the fewest steps: ensure a guest session (mint via the existing guest path if none), pass the one-tap consent + 16+ gate inline if not already accepted, call POST /human/match (US-277), and route to /play/{gameId} when ready (showing a brief loading transition if present - US-279 owns the polished loading screen). The CTA is the visually primary action for a new visitor (spectate/leaderboards remain secondary).
acceptanceCriteria: The home page shows a primary 'Play vs AI' CTA (and a nav entry distinct from the friends-lobby 'Play with friends'); a test asserts the CTA is present and initiates the match flow.

Clicking it takes an unauthenticated first-timer through guest-session ensure -> inline consent + 16+ gate (only if not already accepted) -> POST /human/match -> navigation toward /play/{gameId} (showing a brief loading transition if present); a Playwright e2e test using the established route-mocked fixtures (web/dashboard/tests/e2e play/watch pattern) asserts the navigation reaches the play route for a fresh visitor.
A returning guest (session + consent already present) skips re-consenting and goes straight to match; a test asserts the streamlined path.
The landing page makes 'Play vs AI' the visually primary action while keeping spectate/leaderboard entries; a test asserts the CTA hierarchy.
All frontend gates pass (pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check; e2e where the route-mocked nav test is added). Backend gates pass if client/types touched.
userStories.notes: Sites: web/dashboard/src/routes/+page.svelte (CTA), +layout.svelte (nav), the existing guest-mint + consent flow (reused by friends-lobby join at lobby/[id]/+page.svelte ~207), client.ts (add match() -> POST /human/match). Depends on US-277. Keep consent inline + one-tap. The full live funnel (real game to terminal) is US-287 (needs the US-288 harness); US-278's own nav test can use route-mocked fixtures.

userStories.title: Match loading/queue screen (state, friendly wait, cancel; instant-start on autofill)
userStories.description: When a player hits 'Play vs AI', show feedback while the match is prepared rather than a blank wait or abrupt jump. Add a lightweight loading/queue screen between the CTA and the play surface: reflect match state (preparing/seating/starting), a friendly wait indication, and a cancel/back. Because solo matches autofill AI deterministically, the common path is INSTANT start (no real human queue) - transition immediately to /play/{gameId} when ready, and only show a meaningful wait if admission is briefly deferred (human-lane busy / cost-breaker backoff). Derive a player-appropriate state; do NOT leak operator internals (raw waiting/running counts from /healthz/human-lane).
acceptanceCriteria: A loading/queue screen renders after 'Play vs AI' and before the play surface, showing match state + a cancel/back; a test asserts it appears and that cancel returns the user without a dangling game.
On the common instant-start path (AI autofill succeeds), it transitions promptly to /play/{gameId}; a Playwright e2e (route-mocked) test asserts the fast-path transition.
If admission is briefly deferred, it shows a friendly 'finding your table'/retry state with a bounded wait + working cancel (no infinite spinner); a test asserts the deferred path remains cancelable.
No operator internals leaked to players; a test asserts the player-facing copy.
All frontend gates pass (pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check).
userStories.notes: Sites: a transient loading route/state in web/dashboard/src/routes/play (or the match flow), client.ts match() await/poll, /healthz/human-lane (operator counts - derive a friendly state, do NOT expose raw). Most solo matches start instantly (deterministic autofill). Depends on US-277. Frontend -> pnpm gates (e2e via route-mocked fixtures; no live game needed for US-279's own test).

userStories.title: One-screen 'How to play' onboarding (roles, day/night, win conditions, the spot-the-AI hook)
userStories.description: Nothing teaches a newcomer the game or the core spot-the-AI premise (the product's whole hook). Add a concise, skimmable one-screen 'How to play' explainer: town vs mafia, day discussion + vote, night actions, how a game ends, and the imitation-game twist (some players are AI; at the end you guess which, detection is scored). Reachable from home and surfaced/linkable right before a first match; dismissible/'don't show again' for returning players. Lightweight (no forced multi-step tutorial) and identity-blind-safe (teach the premise without implying mid-game AI/human tells).
acceptanceCriteria: A 'How to play' screen/modal explains town-vs-mafia, day discussion + vote, night actions, win conditions, AND the spot-the-AI premise; a test asserts the key concepts are present.
Reachable from the home page and surfaced/linked before a first-time player's first match; a returning player can skip it (persisted dismissal); a test asserts reachability + skip behavior.
Concise/single-screen and renders correctly on a phone-width viewport; a Playwright e2e test using page.setViewportSize (e.g. 390x844) asserts it renders on mobile.
Copy is identity-blind-safe (no mid-game tells); a content check asserts the framing.
All frontend gates pass (pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check; e2e for the viewport assertion).
userStories.notes: Sites: a new onboarding component/route under web/dashboard/src/routes (linked from +page.svelte and the US-278 match entry). Persist dismissal in localStorage. No Svelte component-mount harness exists (vitest is TS-only) -> use Playwright e2e + page.setViewportSize for render/mobile assertions. Independent of the match endpoint.

userStories.title: In-game rules/help drawer + action tooltips (ruleset-derived: roleblock/track/frame/clean, etc.)
userStories.description: Players forget rules and exotic role actions are opaque. Add an in-game help drawer on the play surface (web/dashboard/src/routes/play/[gameId]/+page.svelte) recapping phases, win conditions, and spot-the-AI scoring, plus short descriptions/tooltips on action affordances for non-obvious actions (roleblock, track, frame, clean, investigate) so a player understands an action before submitting. Descriptions derive from the active ruleset's legal action set (ruleset-by-id; reuse the legal_actions the play surface already consumes), not hardcoded, and stay identity-blind (describe the mechanic, not who can do it).
acceptanceCriteria: The play surface has a help drawer recapping phases, win conditions, and spot-the-AI scoring, reachable without leaving the game; a test asserts it opens/closes.

Non-obvious action affordances show a short description/tooltip derived from the ruleset's legal action set; a test asserts a description is shown for an exotic action (and reflects the ruleset, not a hardcoded list).
Help content is identity-blind and renders on a phone-width viewport; a Playwright e2e test (page.setViewportSize) asserts mobile rendering.
All frontend gates pass (pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check; e2e for the render/viewport assertion). Backend gates if a ruleset action-metadata endpoint is added.
Reducer/derivation of action descriptions (if any pure logic) is unit-tested via vitest over synthetic legal-action payloads.
userStories.notes: Sites: play/[gameId]/+page.svelte + lib components, the legal_actions/observation payload already streamed per seat, core ruleset action definitions. Reuse existing legal_actions. Render/viewport -> Playwright e2e (route-mocked); pure logic -> vitest. Independent of the match flow.

userStories.title: Mobile/portrait responsive play surface (replace the desktop-only 3-column grid)
userStories.description: Mobile is a LAUNCH REQUIREMENT and the play surface is a fixed desktop 3-column grid (play/[gameId]/+page.svelte, md:grid-cols-[220px_1fr_300px]) - unusable on a phone. Re-lay-out responsively/portrait-first: board, chat feed, and action/vote panels all reachable+usable on a phone (e.g. tabbed or stacked on narrow viewports; full layout at md+), touch-sized targets, sane keyboard handling for chat input. Preserve the existing desktop layout at wide breakpoints. This is the structural layout story the other play-surface stories (US-283/284/285) build on (they depend on it).
acceptanceCriteria: On a phone-width viewport (page.setViewportSize ~390x844) the board, chat feed, and action/vote panels are all reachable+usable (tabs or stacked scroll), touch-appropriate; a Playwright e2e/responsive test (route-mocked observation/live SSE fixtures, the play.spec.ts pattern) asserts the key panels are reachable on mobile.
The desktop/wide (md+) 3-column layout is preserved; an e2e test asserts the wide layout renders.
Chat input works with the mobile keyboard (reachable, not obscured; submit works); a test asserts it on a narrow viewport.
No regression to SSE-driven board/chat/action behavior (presentational change); a test asserts live data still renders after the relayout.

All frontend gates pass (pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check + e2e). NOTE: there is NO Svelte component-mount harness (vitest is TS-only) - use Playwright e2e with page.setViewportSize + route-mocked SSE, NOT component tests.
userStories.notes: Sites: play/[gameId]/+page.svelte (md:grid-cols-[220px_1fr_300px]) + lib components. Responsive web only (no native app). Sequence FIRST among play-surface stories. Test via Playwright e2e (page.setViewportSize) + route-mocked SSE fixtures (web/dashboard/tests/e2e/play.spec.ts pattern) - the only render harness that exists.

userStories.title: Live vote-tally panel during the day vote (render the votes the reducer already tracks)
userStories.description: Core Mafia literacy is missing: the play surface shows chat but NO who-voted-for-whom tally, even though the play-state reducer already tracks VoteSubmitted frames. Add a live vote-tally panel shown during the day vote phase rendering current votes (voter -> target, running counts toward elimination) from the reducer's tracked data, updating live as votes stream in. Respect identity-blindness (same anonymized seat representation as the rest of the surface; never an AI/human map) and fit the US-282 responsive layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Vote-tally derivation from the play-state reducer's VoteSubmitted frames (voter->target, running counts) is unit-tested via vitest over synthetic frames (extend playState.test.ts): a test asserts the derived tally reflects seeded votes and updates when a new vote frame is applied.

During the day vote phase the panel renders the derived tally and updates live; a Playwright e2e test with route-mocked SSE vote frames (play.spec.ts pattern) asserts the tally renders and updates on a new mocked vote frame.
The tally uses the identity-blind seat representation (no AI/human disclosure pre-reveal); a test asserts no identity leak.
The panel fits the US-282 responsive layout (usable on a phone-width viewport) and clears/hides outside the vote phase; tests assert mobile rendering + phase-conditioned visibility.
All frontend gates pass (pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check + e2e).
userStories.notes: Sites: play/[gameId]/+page.svelte play-state reducer (already ingests VoteSubmitted) - render what it tracks; SSE live-tail provides vote frames. Depends on US-282. Split: derivation -> vitest (playState.test.ts); render/live-update -> Playwright e2e route-mocked (no live human game exists in the smoke harness).

userStories.title: Core game-feel feedback: confirm-vote, action-accepted toast, eliminated modal, phase banners
userStories.description: Moment-to-moment feedback is thin: one-click votes are unrecoverable with no confirmation; submitted actions give no acceptance feedback; elimination is just a strikethrough; phase changes (day->night) have no announcement. Add: (1) a confirm-vote affordance showing the target before commit (or an undo window); (2) a toast/inline confirmation when an action/vote is accepted by the server; (3) a 'You have been eliminated' modal/state on the player's seat death, with dead-seat visual treatment; (4) phase-change banners ('Night falls'/'Day breaks'). All identity-blind and fitting the US-282 layout.
acceptanceCriteria: Submitting a vote shows a confirmation of the chosen target before commit (or an undo window) so a misclick is recoverable; an e2e test (route-mocked) asserts confirm submits and cancel does not.
An accepted action/vote produces visible acceptance feedback (toast/inline); an e2e test asserts feedback on a successful submit.

When the player's own seat is eliminated, a clear 'You have been eliminated' moment renders and the dead seat is visually distinct; an e2e test (route-mocked elimination frame) asserts the eliminated state.
Phase changes show a banner; derivation of phase-transition from frames is vitest-unit-tested, and an e2e test (route-mocked phase-transition frame) asserts the banner renders.
All feedback is identity-blind and works on a phone-width viewport; tests assert mobile + no identity leak. All frontend gates pass (pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check + e2e).
userStories.notes: Sites: play/[gameId]/+page.svelte + components; phase/elimination/vote frames arrive via SSE + the reducer. Depends on US-282. Keep votes recoverable (no irreversible one-click). Pure phase/elimination derivation -> vitest; render -> Playwright e2e route-mocked (no live human game in the harness).

userStories.title: Connection reliability: LiveClient connection-state callback + backoff + reconnect indicator + return-after-takeover notice
userStories.description: Live play has reliability gaps. The live-tail client (lib liveClient.ts / LiveClient) exposes only onFrame/onError - there is NO onOpen/connected callback, so a 'live'/'recovered' state has nothing to fire on; this story must ADD a connection-state signal (e.g. onOpen/onStateChange emitting live|reconnecting|offline) to LiveClient + the play session, then wire a connection indicator. Also: LiveClient reconnects with no backoff (immediate retry hammering on a flap) - add exponential backoff with cap + jitter. The play surface runs TWO independent streams: the live-tail (LiveClient, reconnect-by-sequence via ?after=<lastSequence> - votes/chat) AND a SEPARATE inline per-seat observation EventSource (obsSse in +page.svelte, half-open + NON-resumable: on error it re-fetches the snapshot next phase tick). AC-1/AC-2 (indicator + backoff) and AC-4 (by-sequence no-dup/no-loss) target the LiveClient live-tail ONLY; obsSse cannot resume by sequence - optionally reflect its state in the indicator + re-fetch with backoff, but do NOT require a by-sequence contract it can't provide. Finally: when a disconnect triggers the server-side silent AI takeover (after grace), a returning player gets a non-leaky 'you were away; resuming' notice that does NOT reveal AI/human identity (anonymous mode).
acceptanceCriteria: LiveClient gains a connection-state callback (onOpen/onStateChange emitting live|reconnecting|offline) wired through the play session; the play surface shows a connection-state indicator reflecting it; an e2e/route-mocked test asserts the indicator reflects a simulated disconnect and recovery.
LiveClient reconnect uses exponential backoff with a cap + jitter (no 0ms retry loop); a vitest unit test over liveClient.test.ts asserts the backoff schedule increases and is bounded.

A player returning after the disconnect grace window sees a non-leaky 'you were away / resuming' notice that does NOT disclose AI/human identity; an e2e test asserts the notice appears on resume and leaks no identity.

AC-4 (live-tail only): reconnect resumes the LiveClient stream by sequence (?after=) with no frame duplication/loss; a vitest test asserts dedup/no-loss across a reconnect. The seat-observation obsSse stream is explicitly OUT of the by-sequence contract (it re-fetches the snapshot); a note/test documents that obsSse recovery is snapshot re-fetch, not sequence-resume.
All frontend gates pass (pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check + e2e). Identity-blind throughout; works on a phone-width viewport.
userStories.notes: Sites: web/dashboard/src/lib/liveClient.ts (ADD onOpen/onStateChange + backoff+jitter; today LiveClientOptions exposes only onFrame/onError and the injected LiveEventSource omits onopen), play/[gameId]/+page.svelte (indicator + return notice + the SEPARATE obsSse EventSource at ~line 110, half-open/non-resumable), the reconnect-by-sequence contract (LiveClient ?after=), the 90s grace/AI-takeover (server-side, identity-blind - do NOT change). Depends on US-282. Split: backoff/dedup logic -> vitest (liveClient.test.ts); indicator/notice render -> Playwright e2e route-mocked.

userStories.title: Profile renders for guests + match-history list + empty/error states
userStories.description: Two fixes so the solo funnel's player can actually see their results. (1) GUEST UN-GATE (BLOCKER): the profile page (web/dashboard/src/routes/profile/+page.svelte) sets signedIn = (me.kind === 'account') and returns early before calling getHumanStats, so a GUEST (the solo funnel mints a guest) is shown the 'Sign in to see your play history' card and NEVER sees stats. Change the page so the stats path runs for ANY authenticated human principal (guest or account) - gate on a successful /human/me session (kind in {guest, account}) rather than account-only; keep a soft 'Sign in to SAVE your history' upsell for guests; only show the signed-out card on a true 401. (2) MATCH HISTORY: profile is aggregate-only; add a bounded/paginated list of the caller's recent games (game_id, ruleset, ended_at, result/outcome, the player's own role + spot-the-AI result where revealed), each linking to that game's reveal/recap, backed by a new endpoint that lists the current human's own games. Add a friendly empty state ('no games yet - Play vs AI') and a graceful error state. Casual + own-games-only (no other players' identities, no pre-reveal leak).
acceptanceCriteria: The profile renders casual stats (US-276) AND match-history for GUEST principals (me.kind === 'guest'), not only accounts; a consented guest with played games sees their stats+history (not the signed-out card); a Playwright e2e test asserts a guest sees stats+history and the 'Sign in to save' upsell, and a true-401 visitor still sees the signed-out card.

A GET endpoint lists the current authenticated human's recent games (bounded/paginated: game_id, ruleset, ended_at, result, the player's own role + spot-the-AI result where revealed); a test asserts the list for a seeded human with multiple games.
The profile renders the match-history list from it, each entry linking to the game's reveal/recap; a test asserts the list renders and links resolve.
Friendly empty state ('no games yet' + 'Play vs AI' CTA) and graceful error state; a test asserts both. Only the caller's own games + already-revealed info are exposed; a test asserts the scoping.
All backend + frontend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration'; pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check + e2e for the guest-render assertion).
userStories.notes: Sites: web/dashboard/src/routes/profile/+page.svelte (line 43 signedIn=me.kind==='account', line 52 'if(!signedIn) return' -> un-gate for guests; lines 80-86 signed-out card -> only on 401), src/padrino/api/routes/human.py (ADD list-my-games route), games/game_seats tables for the caller's principal, reveal/recap routes (play/[gameId]/reveal, watch/[id]/recap) to link to. Backend already serves stats by principal_id (works for guests). Depends on US-276. This story OWNS the profile guest-gate fix (US-276 is backend-only). Casual + own-games-only.

userStories.title: Human-lane e2e harness: deterministic mock-AI seam + run the human-lane worker under smoke
userStories.description: PREREQUISITE for the funnel e2e (US-287). A human-vs-AI game (POST /human/match) is a HUMAN-LANE game (has a HUMAN seat) advanced ONLY by the SEPARATE run_human_lane worker (`padrino human-lane`), NEVER by run_scheduler. Two gaps block an e2e funnel today: (1) the smoke harness (src/padrino/smoke.py run_smoke_subprocess + web/dashboard/tests/e2e/global-setup.ts) spawns only `padrino serve` + `padrino scheduler`, so a /human/match game never advances to terminal; (2) human-lane AI seats use REAL providers by default (run_human_lane's ai_adapter_factory=None builds a real production adapter; run_scheduler's NoopMockAdapter default is NOT shared by the human lane), so there is no deterministic/no-network mock for human games. Fix both: (a) add a settings/env seam (e.g. padrino_human_lane_mock_ai / PADRINO_HUMAN_LANE_MOCK_AI=1) that the `padrino human-lane` CLI reads to inject a deterministic NoopMockAdapter/DeterministicMockAdapter via the existing ai_adapter_factory param (run_human_lane -> build_human_lane_adapter already threads it; the CLI just needs to wire it); (b) add a `--with-human-lane` flag (or equivalent) to `padrino smoke localhost` so run_smoke_subprocess ALSO spawns `padrino human-lane --db-url <same db>` (mock-AI on) as a third keep-running child (mirror the scheduler_proc spawn + _StderrTail + keep_running teardown), and have global-setup.ts enable it. Behind the flag so the existing benchmark smoke gate is unaffected.
acceptanceCriteria: A settings/env flag (padrino_human_lane_mock_ai / PADRINO_HUMAN_LANE_MOCK_AI) makes `padrino human-lane` inject a deterministic mock AI adapter for human-lane AI seats (via the existing ai_adapter_factory seam); a test asserts that with the flag set, a human-lane game's AI seats use the mock adapter (no real provider/network) and the game can be driven to terminal deterministically.

`padrino smoke localhost --with-human-lane --keep-running` spawns the human-lane worker as a third child alongside serve+scheduler (with mock-AI on), and tears it down on exit; a test asserts the worker is started under the flag and that a single-human game created via the match path advances to terminal under it. Without the flag, smoke behavior is unchanged (the benchmark smoke gate is unaffected).

web/dashboard/tests/e2e/global-setup.ts boots the backend with the human-lane worker enabled (so US-287's funnel can run a real human game to terminal); a check asserts global-setup brings up a backend where a human-lane game completes.

Determinism preserved: the mock-AI human game replays bit-for-bit (same seed -> same event hash chain); a test asserts determinism. Firewall intact: the human-lane game writes ZERO canonical scientific rating rows.
All backend + frontend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration'; pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check).
userStories.notes: Sites: src/padrino/cli.py (`human-lane` command ~344-391 - read the mock flag, build ai_adapter_factory), src/padrino/runner/human_lane.py (ai_adapter_factory param already threaded to build_human_lane_adapter; _PlaceholderAdapter docstring ~189-199 notes the real-adapter default), src/padrino/llm/mock NoopMockAdapter/DeterministicMockAdapter, src/padrino/smoke.py run_smoke_subprocess (~849-889 spawn serve+scheduler; ~928-936 teardown - add a third human-lane child behind --with-human-lane), web/dashboard/tests/e2e/global-setup.ts (enable the flag). Priority 287 but runs before US-287 (which depends on it). This is the harness foundation US-287 needs; without it the funnel e2e cannot run a real human game to terminal.

userStories.title: E2E funnel: cold visitor -> Play vs AI -> real human game to terminal -> reveal -> guest profile stats
userStories.description: Lock the headline funnel with an end-to-end test proving a brand-new visitor goes from landing to a finished REAL game to seeing their stats. Using the dashboard e2e harness (Playwright) against the mock-AI backend NOW capable of running human-lane games (US-288), drive: load home -> click 'Play vs AI' -> guest mint + consent inline -> POST /human/match -> loading screen (US-279) -> /play/{gameId} -> play to terminal (the US-288 human-lane worker drives the game with the deterministic mock adapter) -> spot-the-AI reveal -> navigate to /profile and assert GUEST stats render (non-404, US-276 + US-286 guest un-gate) and the match-history (US-286) lists the just-played game. Assert the played game wrote ZERO canonical scientific rating rows (firewall through the live funnel). This is the regression guard that the funnel stays whole and the firewall holds end-to-end.
acceptanceCriteria: An e2e test drives the full cold-visitor funnel (home -> Play vs AI -> guest+consent -> match -> loading -> /play -> terminal -> reveal -> /profile) against the mock-AI backend with the human-lane worker running (US-288), asserting each step reaches the next and the game actually reaches terminal (not a hung CREATED/PENDING game).
The test asserts the /profile GUEST stats render (non-404; US-276 endpoint + US-286 guest un-gate) and the match-history (US-286) lists the just-completed game.
The test runs deterministically with the mock AI adapter (no real provider/network) via the US-288 harness; it fits the existing dashboard e2e gate (global-setup boot).
The test asserts the played game wrote ZERO canonical scientific rating rows (firewall holds through the live funnel) via a real API/DB check in the harness.
All backend + frontend gates pass (ruff/format/mypy/pytest -m 'not integration'; pnpm -C web/dashboard lint + check + the e2e suite).
userStories.notes: Sites: web/dashboard/tests/e2e/* (Playwright; global-setup boots the backend - now with the human-lane worker via US-288), the funnel from US-278 (CTA) + US-277 (match) + US-279 (loading) + US-276/US-286 (guest profile). DEPENDS on US-288 (harness can run a human game to terminal) and US-279 (loading step asserted). This is the guard that the acquisition path never silently breaks again (like /human/stats did). The other play-surface stories use route-mocked fixtures for their own tests; THIS one uses the real human-lane harness to prove the firewall end-to-end.
